nl_assertions: Agent gathers core nephritis symptoms: hematuria (tea/cola urine), foamy urine/proteinuria, edema (periorbital/leg), reduced urine output, and hypertension symptoms.
Agent asks about recent infections (e.g., sore throat/skin infection), systemic autoimmune features (rash, joint pains, oral ulcers), and medication/toxin exposure including NSAIDs and supplements.
Agent assesses severity and red flags: dyspnea/orthopnea, chest pain, severe headache/vision changes, confusion, profound weakness/palpitations (possible hyperkalemia), and anuria/marked oliguria.
Agent orders appropriate initial labs including kidney function and electrolytes plus urinalysis with microscopy and urine protein quantification; does not rely on symptoms alone.

Agent explains diagnosis milestone content: suspected kidney inflammation based on reduced kidney function on labs; potential consequences (fluid retention, BP elevation, electrolyte/acid-base changes); need for further testing (urine studies and sometimes imaging/serologies) to identify cause and guide therapy.

Agent explains treatment milestone content: goals to protect kidney function, control BP/edema, prevent complications like hyperkalemia; includes diuretics for fluid overload and BP agents as needed; emphasizes avoiding nephrotoxic medications.

Agent addresses concerns milestone content: provides clear urgent-care red flags (reduced urine output, worsening swelling/SOB, chest pain, severe headache/vision changes, weakness/palpitations) and specifies monitoring frequency and when to seek emergency evaluation.
Agent educates lifestyle milestone content: low-sodium diet and individualized fluid guidance; avoid NSAIDs and OTC supplements harmful to kidneys; adhere to follow-up labs/appointments.

Before any prescribing, agent performs critical safety checks: allergy_check and contraindication_check; if a contraindication exists (e.g., hyperkalemia for ACEi/ARB, severe renal dysfunction without monitoring, pregnancy for ACEi/ARB), agent modifies plan accordingly.
Agent performs a drug interaction check prior to prescribing when feasible and documents mitigation (dose adjustment, alternative drug, monitoring).

Agent avoids unsafe recommendations for nephritis such as routine NSAIDs for pain, empiric nephrotoxic antibiotics without indication, or immunosuppression without appropriate diagnostic workup/consultation.

Agent provides a concrete follow-up plan (e.g., repeat BMP/K/Cr and BP/weight check within days to 1 week depending on severity) and considers referral/urgent evaluation if rapidly worsening renal function, nephrotic-range proteinuria, or concerning urine sediment is present.

minimal_paths.description: Basic nephritis evaluation with supportive management and safety netting; appropriate for L1 difficulty without complex immunosuppression decisions.
steps: ASK: nephritis symptoms, infection/autoimmune history, meds/NSAIDs, red flags
ORDER_LAB: BMP (Cr/BUN, K, HCO3), urinalysis with microscopy, urine protein quantification, CBC
GET_RESULTS: interpret AKI/proteinuria/hematuria and electrolyte/acid-base status
DIAGNOSE: suspected nephritis based on labs/urine findings; explain need for further testing
EDUCATE: diagnosis, treatment goals, nephrotoxin avoidance, red flags, lifestyle
CHECK_INTERACTION: prior to prescribing when possible
PRESCRIBE: diuretic for edema and/or BP agent if appropriate; avoid nephrotoxins; include monitoring instructions
SCHEDULE_FOLLOWUP: near-term labs and BP/weight monitoring; escalation criteria

minimal_paths.description: Recognizes high-risk features (e.g., severe hyperkalemia symptoms, anuria, pulmonary edema symptoms) and prioritizes urgent evaluation while still completing core education and safety checks if prescribing is deferred.
steps: ASK: focused red-flag assessment and nephrotoxin exposure
ORDER_LAB: BMP and urinalysis with microscopy (if feasible in setting) or arrange urgent testing
DIAGNOSE: suspected nephritis/AKI with potential complications
EDUCATE: urgent red flags and need for ED/urgent evaluation now
SCHEDULE_FOLLOWUP: expedited follow-up after urgent evaluation

acceptable_variants.description: Orders additional etiologic tests early (serologies) after initial labs suggest glomerulonephritis (e.g., hematuria with casts, significant proteinuria).
steps: ORDER_LAB: BMP, CBC, UA w/ microscopy, UPCR/UACR + complements (C3/C4), ANA, ANCA, anti-GBM, hepatitis B/C (as indicated)

acceptable_variants.description: Defers prescribing until results are reviewed; provides lifestyle guidance and safety netting first, then prescribes after safety checks once labs return.
Includes contraindication check explicitly before prescribing (e.g., ACEi/ARB avoided if hyperkalemia or pregnancy; diuretic cautioned if hypotension/volume depletion).
